summary: OATH's own reporting notes this figure fluctuates with hearing volume: decisions issued by Judicial Hearing Officers rose from about 200,000 in FY24 to over 216,000 in FY25 even as average decision time dipped slightly. The rise to 15 days reflected in the FY26 figure tracks a preliminary FY26 report showing decision times climbing from 10 to 14 days in the first four months of the fiscal year alongside a 12 percent increase in decisions issued.

sources.label: OATH, Preliminary Fiscal 2026 Mayor's Management Report

oneLiner: increasing, tracking a rise in hearing volume and decisions issued
